title: Combating Trafficking In Persons General Awareness
source_filename: Combating Trafficking In Persons General Awareness.docx

qas.q: The Trafficking Victims Protection Act of 2000 does which of the following?
Victims of trafficking can be (Select all that apply):
qas.a:
Foreign national or U.S. citizen

Service members, DoD civilians, and DoD contractor employees, DoD family members

Adult or child

Female or male

qas.q: Involuntary servitude and Debt bondage are forms of forced labor.
One method for combating trafficking in persons is to be informed and learn the signs and indicators of trafficking in persons.
Which of the following signs most likely indicates a trafficking in persons situation?
qas.a: A person doesn't have control of their identification documents

qas.q: Which of these impact the U.S. Military's mission and readiness? Select all that apply.
qas.a:
Subcontractor employees working on U.S. government contracts on military installations have been subjected to steep recruitment fees, unsafe working and living conditions, or violence and abuse

Children are used in rogue or extremist militias as soldiers, spies, messengers, guards, and sex slaves

Sex traffickers operate near U.S. military installations to target Service members

Terrorist, organized crime, and extremist groups use trafficking in persons to fund their operations

qas.q: Suspect actions of traffickers include recruitment, harboring, transporting, providing, obtaining (and for sex trafficking patronizing or soliciting prostitution).
Trafficking in persons is not a problem in DoD.
Large scale migration is a factor in the occurrence of trafficking in persons.
At which point should you report a trafficking in persons situation?
Traffickers have sophisticated business models involving new technologies including smartphones, social media, and specialized apps.

You have a responsibility to report any trafficking in persons incidents you may witness, avoid establishments that show indicators of trafficking in persons, and report these establishments to your chain of command.
In actual cases in the U.S. traffickers were (Select all that apply):
qas.a:
-Family members
-Service members, DoD civilians, and DoD contractors, DoD family members
-Business owners
-Members of organized crime or terrorist groups

qas.q: Trafficking in Persons only occurs in poor regions of the world.
Trafficking in persons could take place in which of the following locations?
Any person under the age of 18 found in commercial sex is a per se victim and no proof of force, fraud, or coercion is needed.
Trafficking in persons occurs for many reasons including:

Prosecutable offenses under Article 134 of the UCMJ related to sex trafficking include prostitution, patronizing a prostitute, and pandering by compelling, inducing, enticing, or procuring an act of prostitution.
Traffickers use which of the following tactics to compel a person into commercial sex or forced labor?
Trafficking in persons is a problem in DoD in what following ways? Select all that apply:
qas.a:
-DoD contractors have been investigated for sex and labor trafficking
-DoD members were penalized for being buyers
-DoD members have been identified as victims of human trafficking
-DoD members were prosecuted as traffickers

qas.q: Trafficking in persons consists of which of the following?
Traffickers look for victims in vulnerable situations such as _______.
Sex traffickers are most likely to take advantage of individuals in which of the following locations?
qas.a:
-Dance clubs, strip clubs, and bars
-Massage parlors and spas
-Hospitals
-Museums

qas.q: Which of the following most likely indicates a labor trafficking situation?
qas.a:
-Employees were not given contracts in their native language
-Employees are required to work and live in the same location

qas.q: Which of the following is an appropriate action if you suspect a trafficking in persons violation?
qas.a:
-Contact your chain of command
-Contact the Department of Defense Inspector General (IG)

qas.q: Which of the following is an appropriate action if you come across a trafficking in persons violation?
qas.a: Contact the Department of Defense Inspector General (IG)

qas.q: Children, including the children of military members, may be targets for traffickers online, at schools, or in neighborhoods.
The President's 2012 Executive Order 13627 on trafficking in persons strengthened the government's zero tolerance policy by addressing which of the following groups?
Sex trafficking consists of which of the following? Select all that apply.
qas.a: The recruitment, harboring, transportation, provision, obtaining, patronizing, or soliciting of a person for the purpose of a commercial sex act.
